questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: One mole of $\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{H}_4$ loses 10 moles of electrons to form a new compound $Z$. Assuming that all the nitrogens appear in the new compound, what is the oxidation state of nitrogen in $Z$ ? (There is no change in the oxidation state of hydrogen.)
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{H}_4-10 e^{-} \longrightarrow X^{10+}$ (with two $\mathrm{N}$ atoms)
Total oxidation number of two $\mathrm{N}$ atoms in $\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{H}_4$,
$\begin{aligned} \Rightarrow \quad 2 x+4 & =0 \\ 2 x & =-4\end{aligned}$
$\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{H}_4$ loses 10 electrons, so total oxidation number of two $\mathrm{N}$-atoms increases by 10 , i.e., the total oxidation number of two $\mathrm{N}$-atoms in
$Y=-4+10=+6$
$\therefore$ Oxidation number of each $\mathrm{N}$ atom in
$X^{10+}=+3$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
The equivalent weight of which of the following is maximum?
(Given: atomic weights $\mathrm{Na}=23, \mathrm{Mn}=55, \mathrm{Cr}=52, \mathrm{~K}=$ $19, \mathrm{O}=16, \mathrm{C}=12)$
questions.solution: Equivalent weight $=\frac{\text { Molar mass }}{\mathrm{N}-\text { factor }}$ $=\frac{\text { Molar mass }}{\text { Number of } \mathrm{e}^{-} \text {lost or gained }}$ for, $\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{O}_4 ; \mathrm{E}=\frac{134+36}{2}=85$ for, $\mathrm{KMnO}_4 / \mathrm{H}^{+} ; \mathrm{E}=\frac{158}{5}=31.6$ for, $\mathrm{KMn}_4 / \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} ; \mathrm{E}=\frac{158}{3}=52.66$ for, $\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7 / \mathrm{H}^{+} ; \mathrm{E}=\frac{294}{6}=49$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
From the given reactions identify the disproportionation reaction
i) Cl2(g)+2KI(aq)⟶2KCl(aq)+I2(s)
ii) Cl2(g)+2OH-(aq)⟶ClO-(aq)+Cl-(aq)+H2O(l)
iii) Mg(s)+2HCl(aq)⟶MgCl2(aq)+H2(g)
iv) 2H2O2(aq)⟶2H2O(l)+O2(g)
questions.solution:
The reaction in which one reactant gets oxidized, and the same reactant gets reduced is known as disproportionation reaction. 

Cl02(g)+2KI-1(aq)⟶2KC0l(aq)+I-12(s)

In the above reaction different elements undergoing oxidation and reduction. Hence, It is not a disproportionation reaction. 

Cl02(g)+2OH-(aq)⟶Cl+1O-(aq)+Cl-(aq)+H2O(l)

In the above reaction chlorine is undergoing oxidation as well as reduction. Hence, It is a disproportionation reaction. 

Mg0(s)+2H+1Cl(aq)⟶Mg+2Cl2(aq)+H02(g)

In the above reaction magnesium is undergoing oxidation and hydrogen ion undergoing reduction. Hence, It is not a disproportionation reaction. 

2H2O-12(aq)⟶2H2O-2(l)+O02(g)

In the above reaction peroxide is undergoing oxidation as well as reduction. Hence, It is a disproportionation reaction.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: During a redox titration involving a solution containing <math><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> ions against <math><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><msubsup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo></mrow></msubsup></math> in the presence of excess of <math><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> ions, the number of electrons that gets transferred is
questions.solution:
For redox titration of a solution containing Fe2+ against MnO4-, H+ ions are present in excess i.e., the solution is acidic.

Mn+7O4-+ Fe2+→Fe3++Mn2+  (acidic medium)

Here, oxidation number of Mn is reduced from +7 to +2 (reduction) and oxidation number of Fe is increased from +2 to +3(oxidation).

Reduction half cell,

MnO4-+8H++5e-⟶Mn2++4H2O....(equation 1)

Oxidation half cell,

[Fe2+⟶Fe3++e-]×5.....(equation 2)

By adding equation 1 and 2, final balanced reaction will be,

MnO4-+8H++5Fe2+⟶Mn2++5Fe3++4H2O
Therefore, five electrons gets transferred.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: 1 litre solution of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>KIO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> of unknown molarity is given to titrate with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>KI</mi></math> in strong acid medium. 50 mL solution of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>KIO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> requires 10 mL of 0.1 M <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>KI</mi></math> for complete reduction to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>. The molarity of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>KIO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> solution is :
questions.solution:
1 0 e + 2 I 5 + → I 0 2 2 I - → I 0 2 + 2 e

Meq. of KIO3 = Meq. of I-

            5 0 × M × 5 = 1 0 × 0.1 × 1

∴            M = 1 2 5 0 = 4 × 1 0 - 3
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: One mole of $\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{H}_4$ loses 10 moles of electrons to form a new compound $Z$. Assuming that all the nitrogens appear in the new compound, what is the oxidation state of nitrogen in $Z$ ? (There is no change in the oxidation state of hydrogen.)
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{H}_4-10 e^{-} \longrightarrow X^{10+}$ (with two $\mathrm{N}$ atoms)
Total oxidation number of two $\mathrm{N}$ atoms in $\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{H}_4$,
$\begin{aligned} \Rightarrow \quad 2 x+4 & =0 \\ 2 x & =-4\end{aligned}$
$\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{H}_4$ loses 10 electrons, so total oxidation number of two $\mathrm{N}$-atoms increases by 10 , i.e., the total oxidation number of two $\mathrm{N}$-atoms in
$Y=-4+10=+6$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Which of the following are disproportionation reactions?
(A) $\mathrm{Cl}_2+2 \mathrm{NaOH} \rightarrow \mathrm{NaCl}+\mathrm{NaOCl}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$
(B) $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2 \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{O}_2$
(C) $2 \mathrm{KMnO}_4 \rightarrow \mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{MnO}_4+\mathrm{MnO}_2+\mathrm{O}_2$
(D) $3 \mathrm{MnO}_4^{2-}+4 \mathrm{H}^{+} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}+\mathrm{MnO}_2+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Formic acid is heated with conc. \(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4\) at \(100^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\) to form \(A\) and \(B\). When \(\mathrm{Fe}_2 \mathrm{O}_3\) is heated strongly with \(B\), the products formed are \(C\) and \(D . C\) can also be obtained by reacting \(\mathrm{CaCO}_3\) with dil.HCl. What is \(D\) ?
questions.solution:
\(\mathrm{HCOOH} \stackrel{\text { Conc } \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4}{\longrightarrow} \underset{(B)}{\mathrm{CO}}+\underset{(A)}{\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}}\)
\(\underset{(B)}{3 \mathrm{CO}}+\mathrm{Fe}_2 \mathrm{O}_3 \longrightarrow \underset{(D)}{2 \mathrm{Fe}}+\underset{(C)}{3 \mathrm{CO}_2}\)
Thus, \(D\) is \(\mathrm{Fe}\),
Further,
\(\mathrm{CaCO}_3+2 \mathrm{HCl} \text { (dil.) } \longrightarrow \mathrm{CO}_2+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{CaCl}_2\)
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: On reduction with hydrogen, $3.6 \mathrm{~g}$ of an oxide of metal $(M)$ left $3.2 \mathrm{~g}$ of the metal. If the atomic weight of the metal is 64 . The formula of the oxide is
questions.solution: Moles of $M=\frac{3.2}{64}=\frac{1}{20} \mathrm{~mol}$ Weight of oxygen $=3.6-3.2=0.4$ Moles of oxygen $=\frac{0.4}{16}=\frac{1}{40}$ $$ \begin{aligned} & \frac{\text { Moles of metal }}{\text { Moles of oxygen }}=\frac{\frac{1}{20}}{\frac{1}{40}}=2: 1 \\ & \therefore \quad M=2 \text { and } \\ & \therefore \quad \mathrm{O}=1 \text { or the formula of oxide is } M_2 \mathrm{O} . \end{aligned} $$ Hence, correct option is (b).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: \(20 \mathrm{~mL}\) of \(\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}\) solution of certain concentration has completely reacted with \(20 \mathrm{~mL}\) of \(0.01 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7\) in acidic medium. If \(20 \mathrm{~mL}\) of same \(\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}\) solution has reacted completely with \(20 \mathrm{~mL}\) of \(\mathrm{KMnO}_4\) solution in acid medium, the molarity of \(\mathrm{KMnO}_4\) solution is
questions.solution:
The reaction between \(\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}\) and \(\mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7^{2-}\)
(i.e. \(\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7\)) in acidic medium occurs as follows :
\(\mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7^{2-}+14 \mathrm{H}^{+}+6 \mathrm{Fe}^{2+} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Cr}^{3+}+7 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}+6 \mathrm{Fe}^{3+}\)
The reaction between \(\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}\) and \(\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}\)
(i.e. \(\mathrm{KMnO}_4\)) in acidic medium occurs as follows :
\(\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}+8 \mathrm{H}^{+}+5 \mathrm{Fe}^{2+} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Mn}^{2+}+4 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}+5 \mathrm{Fe}^{3+}\)
\(\therefore \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7^{2-}\) react with \(6 \mathrm{Fe}^{2+} n_1\) and \(\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}\)react with \(5 \mathrm{Fe}^{2+}\left(n_2\right)\) in a balanced equation.
Gram equivalent of \(\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}\)
\(=\) gram equivalent of \(\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7\)
\(=\) gram equivalent of \(\mathrm{KMnO}_4\)
Therefore, on comparing gram equivalent of \(\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7\) and \(\mathrm{KMnO}_4\), we have
\(n_1=6, n_2=5\)
Conc. of \(\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7=0.01 \mathrm{M}\left(M_1\right)\)
Volume of \(\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7=20 \mathrm{~mL}\left(V_1\right)\)
react with \(\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}\) ions \(=6 \mathrm{~mol}\left(n_1\right)\)
Conc. of \(\mathrm{KMnO}_4=\) to find (say \(M_2\))
Volume of \(\mathrm{KMnO}_4=20 \mathrm{~mL}\left(V_2\right)\)
react with \(\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}\) ions \(=5 \mathrm{~mol}\left(n_2\right)\)
Thus, \(\quad M_1 \times V_1 \times n_1=M_2 \times V_2 \times n_2\)
or, \(0.01 \times 20 \times 6=M_2 \times 20 \times 5\)
or \(M_2=\frac{0.01 \times 6}{5}=0.012 \mathrm{M}\)
Hence, option (d) is the correct answer.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Alcohol level in blood can be determined by a redox titration with dichromate solution in acidic medium according to the balanced equation

     C 2 H 5 OH aq + 2 Cr 2 O 7 2 - aq + 1 6 H + ⟶ 2 CO 2 g + 4 Cr 3 + aq + 1 1 H 2 O l⁡

What is blood alcohol level in mass percent if 8.0 mL of 0.05 M Cr 2 O 7 2 -  solution is required for titration of a 10.0 g sample of blood?
questions.solution:
C 2     H 6 O → 2 CO 2 + 4 + 12e - (n Factor = 12) Cr 2 O 7 2 - + 1 4 H + + 6 e - → 2 Cr 3 + + 7 H 2 O (n Factor = 6)

Cr 2 O 7 2 - = 8 × 0.05 × 6 1 0 0 0

              = 0.0024 Eqs

               = Eqs of C 2 H 5 OH

Moles of ethanol = 0.0024 1 2 = 0.0002 moles

                            = 2 × 1 0 - 4 × 4 6 = 9 2 × 1 0 - 4 g

% Ethanol = 9 2 × 1 0 - 4 1 0 × 1 0 0 = 0.092 %
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: How many gram-equivalents of $\mathrm{NaOH}$ are required to neutralise $25 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$ of a decinormal $\mathrm{HCl}$ solution?
questions.solution:
For acid-base reactions, g-equivalent of acid $=$ g-equivalent of base
$$
\begin{aligned}
(\mathrm{HCl}) &(\mathrm{NaOH}) \\
\therefore \text { g-equivalent of acid } &=\left(25 \times 10^{-3}\right) \mathrm{dm}^{3} \times \frac{1}{10} \\
=& 25 \times 10^{-4} \\
\text { or } &=0.0025
\end{aligned}
$$
$=\mathrm{g}$ -equivalent of $\mathrm{NaOH}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: For the redox reaction <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msubsup><mi>MnO</mi><mn>4</mn><mo>-</mo></msubsup><mo>+</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msubsup><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>4</mn><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msubsup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mo>+</mo></msup><mo>→</mo><msup><mi>Mn</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msub><mi>CO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>+</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></math>, the correct coefficients of the reactants for the balanced reaction are respectively <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msubsup><mi>MnO</mi><mn>4</mn><mo>-</mo></msubsup><mo>,</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msubsup><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>4</mn><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msubsup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mo>+</mo></msup></math>:
questions.solution:
For balancing the reaction four steps should be followed in the given order

 1. Balance elements i.e central metal which oxidised or reduced.

 2. Balance oxygen by adding water molecules.

 3. Balance hydrogen by adding H+ ion.

 4. Balance charge by adding electrons.

Balancing of reaction is given below:


2KMnO4​+5H2C2O4+3H2SO4​→K2SO4+2MnSO4​+10CO2​+8H2O
MnO4−=-2
C2O4−2=5
H+=16


Thus, the correct coefficients of MnO4−, C2O4−2 and H+ are 2, 5 and 16 respectively.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following is only a redox reaction but not a disproportionation reaction?
questions.solution:
The reaction $\mathrm{P}_4+8 \mathrm{SOCl}_2 \rightarrow 4 \mathrm{PCl}_3+2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{Cl}_2+4 \mathrm{SO}_2$ involves change of oxidation state of $\mathrm{P}$ from 0 to +3 and that of $S$ from +4 to +2 .

Thus, it is a redox reaction but not a disproportionation reaction.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
What is the mass of $\mathrm{KClO}_{3(\mathrm{~s})}$ required to liberate 22. $4 \mathrm{dm}^3$ oxygen at STP during thermal decomposition?
(Molar Mass of $\mathrm{KClO}_{3(\mathrm{~s})}=122.5 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{mol}$ )
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Equivalent weights of $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$ and $\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7$ in acidic medium are respectively.
(Molecular weight of $\mathrm{KMnO}_4=M_A$ and Molecular weight of $\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7=M_B$ ).
questions.solution:
Given that,
Molecular weight of $\mathrm{KMnO}_4=M_A$
$$
\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}+8 \mathrm{H}^{+}+5 \mathrm{e}^{-} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Mn}^{2+}+4 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}
$$
$\therefore$ Equivalent weight of $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$
$$
=\frac{\text { Molecular weight }}{5}=\frac{M_A}{5}
$$
Similarly, molecular weight of $\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7=M_B$
$$
\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7+14 \mathrm{H}^{+}+6 e^{-} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{~K}^{+}+2 \mathrm{Cr}^{3+}+7 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}
$$
$\therefore$ Equivalent weight of $\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7$
$$
=\frac{\text { Molecular weight }}{6}=\frac{M_B}{6}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
2KMnO4+3H2SO4+5H2O2→K2SO4+2MnSO4+8H2O+5O2
Find the nonnality of H2O2 solution, if 20 mL of it is required to react completely with 16 mL of 0.02MKMnO4 solution. ( Molar mass of KMnO4=158 g mol-1)
questions.solution:
n.f. of H2O2=2

n.f. of KMnO4=5

Number of mequivalents of H2O 2=Number of mequivalents of KMnO4

N1V1=N2V2

N1=N2V2V1=(0.02×5)×1620=0.08 N
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: <math> <mrow> <mtext>6</mtext><mo>×</mo><msup> <mrow> <mtext>10</mtext></mrow> <mrow> <mo>−</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow> </msup> </mrow> </math> mole <math> <mrow> <msub> <mtext>K</mtext> <mtext>2</mtext> </msub> <msub> <mrow> <mtext>Cr</mtext></mrow> <mtext>2</mtext> </msub> <msub> <mtext>O</mtext> <mtext>7</mtext> </msub> </mrow> </math> reacts completely with <math> <mrow> <mtext>9</mtext><mo>×</mo><msup> <mrow> <mtext>10</mtext></mrow> <mrow> <mo>−</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow> </msup> </mrow> </math> mole of <math> <mrow> <msup> <mtext>X</mtext> <mrow> <mtext>n+</mtext></mrow> </msup> </mrow> </math> to give <math> <mrow> <msubsup> <mtext>XO</mtext> <mn>3</mn> <mo>-</mo> </msubsup> </mrow> </math> and <math> <mrow> <msup> <mrow> <mtext>Cr</mtext></mrow> <mrow> <mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow> </msup> <mo>.</mo></mrow> </math> Value of <math> <mtext>n</mtext> </math> will be
questions.solution:
X n + → X + 5 O 3 - + 5 - n e -

Cr 2 O 7 2 - + 1 4 H + + 6 e - → 2 Cr 3 + + 7 H 2 O

Making no. of moles of electrons same for oxidation and reduction reaction

6 × 1 0 - 3 × 6 = 5 - n × 9 × 1 0 - 3

Implies n=1
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which among the following statements is incorrect for manganate and permanganate ions?
options: In both the ions, \(p \pi-d \pi\) bonding is observed between oxygen and manganese.
Permanganate is stronger oxidising agent than manganate.
questions.solution:
\(\Rightarrow\) Tetrahedral \(\Rightarrow d \pi(\mathrm{Mn}) \_p \pi(\mathrm{O})\) is present. \(\Rightarrow\) Paramagnetic, due to presence of one unpaired \(d\)-electron of \(\mathrm{Mn}^{+6}\left(3 d^{\prime}\right)\) of \(\mathrm{MnO}_4^{2-}\). \(\Rightarrow\) Oxidising agent.
\(\Rightarrow\) Tetrahedral \(\Rightarrow d \pi(\mathrm{Mn}) \_p \pi(\mathrm{O})\) is present. \(\Rightarrow\) Diamagnetic, due to the absence of unpaired \(d\)-electron in \(\mathrm{Mn}^{+7}\left(3 d^0\right)\) of \(\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}\). \(\Rightarrow\) Stronger oxidising agent.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A solution of 10 mL <math> <mrow> <mfrac> <mrow> <mtext>M</mtext> </mrow> <mrow> <mn>1</mn> <mn>0</mn> </mrow> </mfrac> <msub> <mtext>FeSO</mtext> <mn>4</mn> </msub> </mrow> </math> was titrated with KMnO<sub>4</sub> solution in acidic medium of KMnO<sub>4</sub> used will be
questions.solution:
2 KMnO 4 + 3 H 2 SO 4 → K 2 SO 4 + 2 MnSO 4 + 3 H 2 O + 5 O

2 FeSO 4 + H 2 SO 4 + O ⟶ Fe 2 SO 4 3 + H 2 O × 5

2 KMnO 4 + 8 H 2 SO 4 + 1 0 FeSO 4 ⟶ Products

           M 1 V 1 n 1 = M 2 V 2 n 2 KMnO 4 FeSO 4

M 1 V 1 = n 1 n 2 M 2 V 2 = 2 1 0 × 1 0 × 1 1 0 = 1 5

for,   M1V1 = 0.02 x 10 = 1/5.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: In redox reaction, <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> oxidises <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msub><mo>[</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><msub><mrow><mo>)</mo></mrow><mrow><mn>6</mn></mrow></msub><mo>]</mo></math> into <math><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msubsup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msubsup></math> and <math><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><msubsup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo></mrow></msubsup></math> ions in acidic medium, than how many moles of <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> will react with <math><mn>1</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi></math> of <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msub><mo>[</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><msub><mrow><mo>)</mo></mrow><mrow><mn>6</mn></mrow></msub><mo>]</mo></math>
options: <math><mn>5 </mn><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math>

<math><mn>9 </mn><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math>

<math><mn>8 </mn><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math>

<math><mn>30.5 </mn><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math>
questions.solution:
n-factor of H2O2=2

n-factor of K4[Fe(CN)6]=61

∵mole of H2O21 mole of K4Fe(CN)6=612

∴  mole of H2O2=612×1=30.5
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: x mole KIO<sub>3</sub> is treated with excess of KI, liberated I<sub>2</sub> which was dissolved in freshly prepared starch solution which was titrated with by 60 mL 0.1 N Na<sub>2</sub>S<sub>2</sub>O<sub>3</sub> until the end point. What is x?
questions.solution:
I - ⟶ 1 2 I 2 + e ] × 5     (HR)

IO 3 - 1 + 6 H + + 5 e → 1 2 I 2 + 3 H 2 O (RHR)

5 I - + I O 3 - + 6 H + ⟶ 3 I 2

I 2 + 2 N a 2 S 2 O 3 ⟶ 2 N a I + N a 2 S 4 O 6

6 0 mL of O . 1 N Na 2 S 2 O 3 = 6 Meqs = 6 mM Na 2 S 2 O 3

≡ 3 mm of I 2

≡ 1 mm of KIO 3 = x

∴ x = 1 0 - 3 moles

questions.q: A <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi>ml</mi></math> solution of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>1</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mo> </mo><mi>HCl</mi></math> was titrated with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.2</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mo> </mo><mi>NaOH</mi></math> solution. The titration was discontinued after adding <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>30</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi>ml</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NaOH</mi></math> solution. The remaining titration was completed by adding <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.25</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mo> </mo><mi>KOH</mi></math> solution. The volume of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>KOH</mi></math> required for completing the titration is
questions.solution:
In the neutralization of acid and base N×V of both must be equivalent

N×V of HCl = 0.1×100 = 10

N×V of NaOH= 0.2×30=6

N1V1ml=N×VNaOHml+N×VmlKOH

0.1×100 = 0.2×30+0.25×V

10 = 6+0.25 V

V=40025= 16 ml
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
A student made the following observations in the laboratory.

I. Clean copper metal did not react with 1molar PbNO32 solution

II. Clean lead metal dissolved in a 1 molar AgNO3 solution and crystals of Ag metal appeared

III. Clean silver metal did not react with 1 molar CuNO32 solution

The order of decrease in reducing character of three metals is
questions.solution:
Metal placed above in electrochemical series replaces the other from its salt solution which is lower in the series.

The low Eo value containing metals have the high reducing character.

Thus, order of reducing character of three metals is

Pb>Cu>Ag
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Hot concentrated sulphuric acid is a moderately strong oxidizing agent. Which of the following reactions does not show oxidizing behaviour?
questions.solution:
CaF2+H2SO4→CaSO4+2HF

In this reaction, there is no change in oxidation state of any atom.

questions.q: 18.3 g of a pure monobasic organic acid containing two 'O' atoms per molecule is burnt completely in excess of oxygen and CO<sub>2</sub> evolved is absorbed completely in one litre of an aqueous solution of NaOH. A 10 mL portion of this solution required 14.5 mL of a normal HCl solution to reach the phenolphthalein end point. An another 10 mL portion of the same solution required 25 mL of the same HCl solution to reach the methyl orange end point. If the organic acid contains 26.23% oxygen by weight, deduce the strength of original NaOH solution.
questions.solution:
Reaction of CO2 & NaOH

2NaOH+CO2⟶Na2CO3+H2O

Let 10 mL final solution has x mM Na2CO3  and y mM of NaOH

y + x = 14.5  (Phenolphthalein)

y + 2x = 25.0 ( Methyl orange)

Total mM of Na2CO3 Produced  = 1 0 . 5 × 1 0 0 = 1 0 5 0  mM

= mM of CO2

x=10.5

y=4

2NaOH  2×10.5 +  CO 2 10.5 →N a 2 C O 3  + H 2 O

∴Initial NaOH in 10ml=21+4=25m mole

Normality=2510=2.5N
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Match the reactions given in column - I with the nature of the reaction/products given in column - II

Column - I Column - II A MnO 4 2 - aq + 4 H + aq ⟶ p Show disproportionation B Cu 2 + aq + I - aq ⟶ q Redox reaction C CrO 4 2 aq + H + aq ⟶ r One of the products is diamagnetic D Fe 3 + aq + Fe CN 6 4 - aq ⟶ s One of the products has metal-oxygen-metal bond/linkage
options: <math> <mrow> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>A</mtext> <mo> → </mo> <mtext>p, q, r</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> <mtext>; </mtext> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>B</mtext> <mo> → </mo> <mtext>q, r</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> <mtext>; </mtext> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>C</mtext> <mo> → </mo> <mtext>r, s</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> <mtext>; </mtext> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>D</mtext> <mo> → </mo> <mtext>r</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> </mrow> </math>

<math> <mrow> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>A</mtext> <mo> → </mo> <mtext>p, q, </mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> <mtext>; </mtext> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>B</mtext> <mo> → </mo> <mtext>q, r</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> <mtext>; </mtext> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>C</mtext> <mo> → </mo> <mtext>r, s, s</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> <mtext>; </mtext> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>D</mtext> <mo> → </mo> <mtext>r</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> </mrow> </math>

<math> <mrow> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>A</mtext> <mo> → </mo> <mtext>p, q, r</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> <mtext>; </mtext> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>B</mtext> <mo> → </mo> <mtext>q, r</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> <mtext>; </mtext> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>C</mtext> <mo> → </mo> <mtext>r, s</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> <mtext>; </mtext> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>D</mtext> <mo> → </mo> <mtext>s</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> </mrow> </math>

<math> <mrow> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>A</mtext> <mo> → </mo> <mtext>p, q, r</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> <mtext>; </mtext> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>B</mtext> <mo> → </mo> <mtext>q, r, s</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> <mtext>; </mtext> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>C</mtext> <mo> → </mo> <mtext>r, s</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> <mtext>; </mtext> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>D</mtext> <mo> → </mo> <mtext>s</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> </mrow> </math>
questions.solution:
When oxidation and reduction take place on the same element of a compound is called a disproportionation reaction. 

The chemical reaction which involves transfer of electrons from one chemical substance to another is called a redox reaction.

A species which has no unpaired electron is called as diamagnetic.

(A) Mn VI O 4 2 - + 4 H + ⟶ 2 Mn VII O 4 - + Mn IV O 2 + 2 H 2 O

The oxidation state of Mn is changing from +6 to +7 and +6 to +4. Hence it is a disproportionation reaction, redox reaction and MnO4- is diamagnetic in nature.

(B) +2       -1   +1 -1    0  2Cu2++4I-→Cu2I2↓+I2

It is a redox reaction and Cu2I2 is diamagnetic in nature.

(C) 2CrO42-+2H+→Cr2O72-+H2O

Cr2O72- is diamagnetic in nature and it also has Cr-O-Cr linkage.

(D) 4Fe3++3FeCN64-→Fe4FeCN63

Fe4FeCN63 is diamangnetic in nature.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: An impure sample of sodium oxalate <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><msub><mi>Na</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mo>)</mo><mo> </mo></math>weighing <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>20</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> is dissolved in an aqueous solution of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>SO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math>and the solution is titrated at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>70</mn><mo>°</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math>, requiring <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>45</mn><mo> </mo><mi>ml</mi></math> <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>02</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mo> </mo><msub><mi>KMnO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math> solution. The endpoint is overrun, and the back titration is carried out with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi>ml</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi></math> oxalic acid solution. Find the per cent purity of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Na</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math> in the sample.
questions.solution:
The number of milli equivalent of Na2C2O4 = milli equivalent of KMnO4 reacted

Total milli equivalent of KMnO4 - excess milli equivalent of KmnO4 reacts with H2C2O4

=45×0.02×5-10×0.1×2=2.5

1000×W134×2 =2.5

WNa2C2O4=0.1675g

Percent purity of Na2C2O4in sample= 0.1675 0.2×100=83.75
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of a solution containing <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Na</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>CO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>NaHCO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> is titrated by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>HCl</mi></math> using phenolphthalein and then, methyl orange (added after first endpoint). The first and second endpoints were found after adding <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mo>/</mo><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi>HCl</mi></math>, respectively. The ratio of meq of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Na</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>CO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>NaHCO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> in the solution is
questions.solution:
Let the meq of Na2CO3 and NaHCO3 are a and b, respectively.

Reactions taking place are as follows.

With phenolphthalein,

HCl+Na2CO3→NaHCO3+NaCl

This gives, meq of HCl=meq of Na2CO3

10×110=a  …i

Later, with methyl orange,

HCl+NaHCO3→H2CO3+NaCl

This gives, meq of HCl=total meq of NaHCO3 

15×110=a+b  …ii

On solving the two equations, we get,

a=1; b=0.5Required ratio of meq=2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Disproportionation products of one mole of $\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-2}$ in aqueous acidic medium are
questions.solution:
$2 e^{-}+4 \mathrm{H}^{+}+\mathrm{MnO}_4^{2-} \longrightarrow \mathrm{MnO}_2+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$
$$
\left(\mathrm{MnO}_4^{2-} \longrightarrow \mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}+e^{-}\right) \times 2
$$

Overall reaction,
$$
3 \mathrm{MnO}_4^{2-}+4 \mathrm{H}^{+} \longrightarrow \mathrm{MnO}_2+2 \mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}
$$
$\because 3 \mathrm{~mol} \mathrm{MnO}_4^{2-}$ gives $1 \mathrm{~mol} \mathrm{MnO}_2$
$\therefore 1 \mathrm{~mol} \mathrm{MnO}{ }_4^{2-} \longrightarrow \frac{1}{3} \mathrm{~mol}$ of $\mathrm{MnO}_2$
Moreover,
$3 \mathrm{~mol}$ of $\mathrm{MnO}_4^{2-}$ gives $2 \mathrm{~mol} \mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}$
$\therefore 1 \mathrm{~mol} \mathrm{MnO}_4^{2-} \longrightarrow \frac{2}{3} \mathrm{~mol} \mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}$
Hence, option (b) is correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Chlorine oxidises sulphur dioxide in the presence of water to give an oxyacid $A$. Chlorine also oxidises iodine in the presence of water to give an oxyacid $B$. The oxidation states of $\mathrm{S}$ and I in $A$ and $B$ are respectively
questions.solution:
(i) When chlorine oxidises sulphur dioxide in presence of water, it gives $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ as oxyacid $(A)$. The reaction occurs as follows :
$$
\mathrm{Cl}_2+\mathrm{SO}_2+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4+2 \mathrm{HCl}
$$

The oxidation number of sulphur in $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ is
$$
2+x+(4 x-2)=0
$$
or, where $x=$ oxidation state of sulphur (S).
$$
x+2-8=0 \Rightarrow x=+6
$$

Hence, oxidation state of (S) in $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ is (+) 6 .
(ii) When chlorine $\left(\mathrm{Cl}_2\right)$ oxidises iodine $\left(\mathrm{I}_2\right)$ in presence of water, it gives $\mathrm{HIO}_3$ as oxyacid $(B)$, the reaction occurs as follows:
$$
5 \mathrm{Cl}_2+\mathrm{I}_2+6 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{HIO}_3+10 \mathrm{HCl} \text {. }
$$

The oxidation state of (I) in $\mathrm{HIO}_3$ is :
Let oxidation state of $(\mathrm{I})=x$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& 1+x+3 \times(-2)=0 \\
& x+1-6=0 \Rightarrow x=+5
\end{aligned}
$$

Hence, oxidation state of iodine (I) in $\mathrm{HIO}_3$ is $=(+) 5$

Hence, option (c) is the correct answer.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In which of the following transformations, oxygen is not the reducing agent?
questions.solution:
A reducing agent oxidises itself or liberates electrons for other reagents.
In the oxidation reaction, the oxidation number increases. In this reaction: 4 NH3+3O2→2N2+6H2O 

The oxidation number of oxygen in O2 is zero and in H2O oxidation number of oxygen is -2. Oxygen is reduced, so O2 acts as an oxidising agent and does not act as reducing agent.

Ag2O→2Ag+12O2

In this reaction, oxidation number of oxygen changes from -2 to 0. Here, oxidation takes place and oxygen acts as a reducing agent.

2F2+2H2O→4HF+H2O

In this reaction, the oxidation number of oxygen remains same. So, oxygen is neither an oxidizing agent nor reducing agent.

AgNO3+H2O2→2Ag+2HNO3+O2 

In this reaction, the oxidation number of oxygen changes from -1 to 0. It shows the oxidation of oxygen and here, oxygen acts as a reducing agent.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Identify the oxidation states of $\mathrm{Mn}$, when $\mathrm{MnO}_4^{2-}$ ion undergoes disproportionation reaction under acidic medíum.
questions.solution:
The disproportionation reaction for $\mathrm{MnO}_4^{2-}$ occurs as follows :
$$
3 \stackrel{+6}{\mathrm{MnO}_4^{2-}}+4 \mathrm{H}^{+} \longrightarrow \stackrel{+4}{\mathrm{MnO}_2}+2 \stackrel{+7}{\mathrm{M} n} \mathrm{MO}_4^{-}+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}
$$
Hence, $\mathrm{MnO}_4^{2-}$ is oxidised to $\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}$and reduced to $\mathrm{MnO}_2$. Thus, oxidation state of $\mathrm{Mn}$ when $\mathrm{MnO}_4^{2-}$ undergoes disproportionation reaction under acidic medium are +7 and +4 .
$\therefore$ Hence option (d) is the correct answer.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
In the following reaction mixtures,
I. $\mathrm{Cu} / \mathrm{CuSO}_4+\mathrm{Ag} / \mathrm{Ag}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$
II. Zn $/ \mathrm{ZnSO}_4+\mathrm{Cu} / \mathrm{CuSO}_4$
copper respectively undergoes
questions.solution: So, the reactions can be written as, (i) $\stackrel{0}{\mathrm{C}} \mathrm{u}+\stackrel{+1}{\mathrm{Ag}} \mathrm{SO}_2 \longrightarrow \stackrel{+2}{\mathrm{Cu}} \mathrm{SO}_4+2 \stackrel{0}{2 \mathrm{Ag}}$ (ii) $\stackrel{0}{\mathrm{Zn}}+\stackrel{+2}{\mathrm{Cu}} \mathrm{SO}_4 \longrightarrow \stackrel{+2}{\mathrm{ZnSO}_4}+\stackrel{0}{\mathrm{C} u}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Why does the following reaction occur ?

           XeO64-aq+2F-aq+6H+aq⟶XeO3g+F2g+3H2Ol⁡

What conclusion about the compound Na4XeO6  (of which XeO 6 4 - is a part) can be drawn from the reaction ? select any one of the correct choice.
options: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>[</mo><msub><mi>XeO</mi><mn>6</mn></msub><msup><mo>]</mo><mrow><mn>4</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msup></math> is a stronger oxidising agent than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>
questions.solution:
Oxidation number change for the compounds in the reaction.

Xe+8O64-aq⁡+2F⁡-1-1aq⁡+6H+aq⁡⟶Xe+6O3g+F20+3H2Ol⁡

In the above reaction:

  • Oxidation number of Xe in XeO 6 4 - decreases from +8 to +6 in XeO3
  • Oxidation number of F increases from -1 (in F-) to zero (in F2).

Hence, XeO 6 4 - or Na4XeO6 is reduced and F- is oxidised.

This reaction occurs because Na4XeO6 or XeO 6 4 - is a stronger oxidising agent than fluorine.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
a H2O2+O3→H2O+2O2
b H2O2+Ag2O→2Ag+H2O+O2
Role of hydrogen peroxide in the above reactions is respectively:
options: Oxidizing in <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> and reducing in <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>

reducing in <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> and oxidizing in <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>
questions.solution:
O3 is reduced into O-2 ion and
Ag2O is reduced to Ag so
H2O2 is reducing agent in both a and b
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following reaction is a redox reaction ?
questions.solution:
Redox reaction is a reaction in which both oxidation and reduction occurs simentaneously.

  • Oxidation is gain in oxidation number
  • Reduction is loss in oxidation number.

1) Na +1Br-1 + H +1Cl-1 → Na +1Cl-1 + H +1Br-1

there is no change in the oxidation numbers taking place, so no oxidation and no reduction in this reaction.

2) H +1Br-1 + Ag +1N+5 O-23 → Ag +1Br-1 + H+1 N +5O-23

there is no change in the oxidation numbers taking place, so no oxidation and no reduction in this reaction.

3) H20 + Br20 → 2H+1Br-1

  • oxidation number of hydrogen is increasing hence oxidation is taking place.
  • oxidation number of bromine is decreasing hence reduction is taking place.

so the reaction is redox.

4) Na2+1O-2 + H2 +1S +6O4-2 → Na2 +1S +6O4-2 + H2+1O-2

there is no change in the oxidation numbers taking place, so no oxidation and no reduction in this reaction.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Consider the reaction :

H 2 SO 3 aq + Sn aq 4 + + H 2 O l⁡ → Sn aq 2 + + HSO 4 aq - + 3 H aq +

Which of the following statements is correct ?
options: Sn<sup>4</sup><sup>+</sup> is the oxidizing agent because it undergoes oxidation
Sn<sup>4</sup><sup>+</sup> is the reducing agent because it undergoes oxidation
H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>3</sub> is the reducing agent because it undergoes reduction
H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>3</sub> is the reducing agent because it undergoes oxidation
questions.solution:
Identify oxidation states of all elements in Redox Reaction

H2SO+43+Sn4++H2O(l)→Sn2++HSO4- +6 +3H+

S is changing the oxidation state from (+4) to (+6). i.e H2SO3 is a Reducing agent because it undergoes oxidation.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
